title: China-to-Philippines Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 60 Hz Grid Re-Validation for Philippine Deployment
rows.target_requirement: The Philippine power system operates at 60 Hz. PV inverter models configured and tested mainly for China 50 Hz operation should be re-validated for Philippine 60 Hz operation, including frequency protection thresholds, reconnection delay, anti-islanding detection, phase-lock behavior, thermal performance, label ratings, and firmware parameter locks used for the local grid profile.
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic PV inverter tests are typically built around 50 Hz grid operation. A China market certificate, datasheet, or GB/T declaration may not show that the same hardware, firmware, relays, magnetics, thermal design, and grid-profile lock have been validated for 60 Hz Philippine settings.
rows.gap: Provide a Philippine grid profile or utility-specific profile, firmware version matrix, 60 Hz type-test or engineering validation evidence, anti-islanding results at 60 Hz, and nameplate / manual values that do not conflict with the model imported into the Philippines.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a practical utility-acceptance and commissioning requirement for Philippine grid connection, even where it is documented through project files rather than a standalone product certificate
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic baseline is generally 50 Hz; 60 Hz validation is export-market and project-specific

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Re-check the inverter as a 60 Hz Philippine grid product. A 50 Hz China configuration is not enough evidence for local utility acceptance without 60 Hz settings, tests, and commissioning controls.

source.publisher: National Grid Corporation of the Philippines (NGCP)

rows.requirement: BPS / PNS Safety Evidence and IEC 62109 for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: The Bureau of Philippine Standards maintains the Philippines list of products under mandatory certification. As checked for this dataset, that public BPS mandatory list should be used to confirm whether the exact inverter model, imported package, plug, cable, switchgear, or other bundled electrical component is in scope. IEC 62109 is the international safety series for power converters used in photovoltaic power systems and may be requested as PNS / IEC safety evidence by buyers, utilities, engineers, or project owners, but the public BPS list should not be read as a blanket mandatory whole-unit certification rule for every PV inverter without a product-specific scope citation.
target_standards: BPS list of products under mandatory certification
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 37408 for photovoltaic grid-connected inverter technical requirements and IEC 62109 CB Scheme reports for export safety evidence. GB/T evidence and IEC CB reports can support Philippine due diligence, but they do not by themselves determine BPS mandatory-scope status or replace any requested PNS / BPS confirmation.
rows.gap: Do not assume that a Chinese IEC 62109 report or GB/T declaration closes Philippine market review. Confirm BPS mandatory scope against the exact HS code, product description, and bundled components; keep IEC 62109-1:2010/-2 test reports, critical component lists, labels, manuals, and Philippine-language importer declarations ready for utility, EPC, buyer, or BPS review.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only for products or bundled components that BPS identifies as in mandatory certification scope; IEC 62109 / PNS evidence may be voluntary, project-specific, utility-requested, or buyer-requested where no mandatory BPS instrument is cited
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or export-supporting for GB/T and IEC CB evidence unless a separate Chinese domestic rule applies

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat BPS scope as product-specific. IEC 62109 evidence is important for PV inverter safety due diligence, but do not call the whole inverter unit mandatory-certified in the Philippines unless BPS scope is confirmed for the exact product.

source.publisher: Bureau of Philippine Standards (BPS), Department of Trade and Industry

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline vs Philippine Acceptance Package
rows.target_requirement: Philippine acceptance is built from product-scope review, project interconnection, utility settings, and buyer or engineer documentation. A China GB/T baseline is useful engineering evidence, but it is not a Philippine approval route and should be mapped clause-by-clause to BPS scope, IEC 62109 safety evidence, ERC / DOE net-metering files, Distribution Code settings, 60 Hz validation, and EMC evidence.
target_standards: BPS mandatory certification scope where applicable
ERC / DOE net-metering and interconnection requirements
rows.china_equivalent: The common China baseline for grid-connected PV inverters includes GB/T 37408 for inverter technical requirements and related GB/T grid-connection documents such as GB/T 19964. These are recommended or technical standards used for China-side conformity evidence and engineering design, not automatic Philippine market authorizations.
rows.gap: Build a crosswalk rather than a one-for-one certificate substitution. The Philippine file should identify which China GB/T tests support each local requirement and where new evidence is needed, especially BPS scope confirmation, 60 Hz settings, local utility interconnection data, labels and manuals, radio-module approval, and commissioning records.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the underlying Philippine route applies, such as BPS mandatory scope or utility interconnection; the crosswalk itself is a due-diligence tool
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T baseline evidence is generally voluntary or project-specific for export support unless a separate Chinese domestic obligation applies

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use China GB/T documents as a technical baseline, not as a Philippine approval. The practical deliverable is a mapped evidence file showing which GB/T, IEC, EMC, and grid-setting documents satisfy each BPS, ERC / DOE, utility, and buyer request.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR), China National Public Service Platform for Standards Information

rows.target_requirement: Solar PV inverter installations connected to a Philippine distribution system must satisfy the distribution utility's technical review under the Philippine Distribution Code framework. For inverter suppliers, this means documenting protection functions, voltage and frequency operating windows, disconnection behavior, grounding interface, harmonic contribution, power quality, and commissioning checks in a format usable by the utility or project engineer.
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-code documentation commonly references GB/T 19964 and GB/T 37408 for photovoltaic generation and inverter behavior. These are useful engineering baselines, but the Philippine distribution utility will evaluate the actual project against local distribution-code settings and procedures.
rows.gap: The key gap is settings translation. Exporters should provide editable grid-code parameter sheets, test evidence for voltage / frequency ride-through or trip behavior where relevant, harmonic and flicker evidence, anti-islanding certificates, and commissioning instructions aligned to the Philippine utility's requested format.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for interconnection to a Philippine distribution system where the distribution utility applies the Distribution Code and its interconnection process
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific for Chinese domestic grid connection; not a substitute for Philippine utility acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Distribution Code acceptance is site and utility specific. China GB/T grid documents are useful inputs, but the inverter settings package must be reworked for the Philippine distribution utility review.

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence for PV Inverters and Associated Communications
rows.target_requirement: PV inverter projects commonly request EMC evidence covering conducted and radiated disturbance, immunity, and power-quality effects. CISPR 11 / IEC 61000-family reports are typical technical evidence for industrial, scientific, and medical equipment disturbance and immunity assessments. If the inverter includes Wi-Fi, cellular, Bluetooth, or other radio communications, separate Philippine radio or telecom equipment approval may also be relevant for the communication module.
target_standards: Philippine radio / telecom equipment approval where radio modules are included
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese export files may include GB/T EMC reports, IEC 61000-family reports, or EU / UK / AU EMC reports. These can support Philippine review, but the report scope must match the exact inverter model, enclosure, cable configuration, communications module, firmware, and grid profile shipped to the Philippines.
rows.gap: The usual gap is model and configuration coverage. Confirm whether the EMC report includes the same power rating, enclosure, cable lengths, communications accessories, filters, firmware, and installation mode. For radio modules, check whether the module already has Philippine approval or whether the importer must handle local radio documentation.

mandatory_status.target: Usually project, buyer, utility, or module-specific unless a cited Philippine instrument makes the exact product or radio module mandatory; radio communications may trigger separate mandatory approval
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary, export-supporting, or mandatory only where a specific Chinese EMC or radio rule applies

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EMC evidence should be treated as configuration-specific. A generic China or EU EMC report may help, but Philippine project review needs reports that match the actual shipped inverter and any radio communications module.

rows.requirement: ERC / DOE Net-Metering and Distributed Renewable Energy Connection
rows.target_requirement: The Renewable Energy Act establishes net-metering for qualified end-users and requires distribution utilities to enter into net-metering agreements with qualified customers under rules issued by the Energy Regulatory Commission. For inverter exports, the compliance issue is not only product safety: the project package must support the customer's ERC / DOE / distribution-utility interconnection review, metering arrangement, anti-islanding evidence, protection settings, single-line diagram, and commissioning records.
target_standards: Republic Act No. 9513, Renewable Energy Act of 2008
ERC net-metering rules for qualified end-users
DOE renewable energy net-metering implementation guidance
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connected PV inverter documentation commonly includes GB/T grid-support and anti-islanding evidence, Chinese nameplate data, and 50 Hz grid settings. That documentation helps technically, but it must be translated into the Philippine project context and cannot replace the customer-side net-metering agreement, distribution-utility approval, or ERC / DOE documentation route.
rows.gap: The exporter should provide Philippine-ready grid-interconnection documents, not just a factory test report. Typical gaps include local 60 Hz settings, anti-islanding trip data, export-limit functions where requested, wiring and protection drawings, utility application data, and commissioning evidence acceptable to the distribution utility and the qualified end-user's net-metering process.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for customer projects using Philippine net-metering or distribution-utility interconnection; not a standalone factory product certificate
mandatory_status.cn: Project-specific for Chinese interconnection; Chinese factory evidence is supporting documentation only for Philippine net-metering

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Philippine net-metering is a project and customer-utility pathway. A China factory certificate does not close ERC / DOE / distribution-utility interconnection review unless the inverter evidence is packaged for the Philippine net-metering file.

source.publisher: The Lawphil Project / Philippine legal text of Republic Act No. 9513
